channel.title: vulnhub on ezekiel's blog
channel.description: Recent content in vulnhub on ezekiel's blog

item.title: Write-up | Basic Pentesting: 2
item.description: Ce write-up couvrira la machine basic_pentesting_2, dont le .ova est trouvable sur VulnHub. Reconnaissance $ nmap -sV -sC 192.168.1.220 -oN scans/ports.txt ... PORT STATE SERVICE VERSION 22/tcp open ssh OpenSSH 7.2p2 Ubuntu 4ubuntu2.4 (Ubuntu Linux; protocol 2.0) | ssh-hostkey: | 2048 db:45:cb:be:4a:8b:71:f8:e9:31:42:ae:ff:f8:45:e4 (RSA) | 256 09:b9:b9:1c:e0:bf:0e:1c:6f:7f:fe:8e:5f:20:1b:ce (ECDSA) |_ 256 a5:68:2b:22:5f:98:4a:62:21:3d:a2:e2:c5:a9:f7:c2 (ED25519) 80/tcp open http Apache httpd 2.4.18 ((Ubuntu)) |_http-server-header: Apache/2.4.18 (Ubuntu) |_http-title: Site doesn't have a title (text/html). 139/tcp open netbios-ssn Samba smbd 3.

item.title: Write-up | Basic Pentesting: 1
item.description: Ce write-up est dédié à la box basic_pentesting_1. J’ai récupéré le .ova sur VulnHub, mais elle est également accessible sur Root Me. Synopsis : This is a small boot2root VM I created for my university’s cyber security group. It contains multiple remote vulnerabilities and multiple privilege escalation vectors. I did all of my testing for this VM on VirtualBox, so that’s the recommended platform. I have been informed that it also works with VMware, but I haven’t tested this personally.
